svg.text: THE TWO INHERITANCES: THE PARABLE OF THE LEFTOVER FOOD
When offered, eats the leftover food,
and passes the day and night neither hungry nor weak
does not eat the leftover food
this choice leads for a long time to …
When the Teacher lives in seclusion, how should the disciple dwell?
① Does not train in seclusion (vivekaṁ nānusikkhati)
② Does not abandon the mental qualities that the Teacher
③ Indulgent and lax, leader in backsliding, neglectful of seclusion
bāhuliko ca, sāthaliko, okkamane pubbaṅgamo, paviveke nikkhittadhuro
② Abandons the mental qualities that the Teacher tells them to abandon
③ Not indulgent, not lax, not a backslider, leading in seclusion
na ca bāhuliko hoti, na sāthaliko okkamane nikkhittadhuro paviveke pubbaṅgamo
A standard for all disciples: elder (thero) · intermediate (majjhimo) · novice (navo)
lobho ca pāpako doso ca pāpako
kodho ca pāpako upanāho ca pāpako
makkho ca pāpako paḷāso ca pāpako
thambho ca pāpako sārambho ca pāpako
māno ca pāpako atimāno ca pāpako
mado ca pāpako pamādo ca pāpako
"There is a Middle Way for the abandoning of greed and aversion … vanity and negligence,
which leads to tranquility, to direct knowledge, to full awakening, to Nibbāna."
